structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Dehumidification Suitable Facilities
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Examples include facilities handling hygroscopic materials, film drying, manufacturing of candy, chocolate, chewing gum, drugs, chemicals, plastics, packaging moisture-sensitive products, electronics, and supermarkets.

evidence_text:

a n d  D eh u m id ific a tion
GE N E RAL D E SCRI P TI O N
Ther e are two basic types of  open-cycle desiccant process:  solid  and
liq uid  desiccant.  Each of  these pr ocesses has several f orms,  and these
should be investigated to deter mine the most appropriate f or  the partic-
ular application.  B oth of  these systems need to have the air  being con-
ditioned  come in good contact with the d esiccant,  during which
moisture is absor bed  f rom the air and the temper atures of  both the air
and  d esiccant are coincid ently raised .
The moistur e absorption process is caused by desiccant having a
lower surf ace vapor pr essur e than the air.  As the temperature of  the
desiccant rises,  its vapor pressure r ises,  and its usef ul absorption capa-
bility lessens.  S ome systems,  particular ly liq uid  types,  have cooling of
air and desiccant coincident with dehumidif ication.
judge_reason: Evidence points to general description, not the specific list of facilities; summary appears to combine content from multiple pages but not clearly supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Solar Combi Systems
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Combined production of DHW and space heating (solar combi systems) includes solar collectors, heat storage tank, and a boiler as auxiliary heater; requires larger collector area than DHW alone.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   1 1 3

G E N E R AL D E S C R I P TI O N
Dynamic ar chitectural glazing uses electrochromic technology to
dar k en the interior of  a piece of  glass,  enabling a thin coating to change
f r om clear to tinted when small electr ical voltage is applied.  A demon-
str ation of  this technology is eyeglasses that darken when one goes out-
doors.  Dynamic glass f or architectural applications can tr ansf or m into
f our  dark ness levels d epend ing on the season,  being dar ker  in summer
to keep solar gain out and minimize glare .  In ad dition,  because the
glazing becomes d ark er  in the summer when exposed  to sun,  its use
will generally minimize r adiation heat tr ansf er and help impr ove ther -
mal comf ort in the space, .  Typically,  the tint corr espond s to 4%,  20%,
40%,  and  60% visible light transmittance T
vis  d epending on manuf ac-
turers.
judge_reason: Evidence provided is about dynamic glazing, not solar combi systems; no support from source pages 1-5.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Operation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants like silica gel; humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while another part is dried by reactivation air; the desiccant rotates slowly between airstreams.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Regeneration Methods
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Desiccant redrying (regeneration) can be either direct (contact with heated outdoor air) or indirect; indirect may be preferable in high humidity climates.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Process Air Outlet Temperature Rise
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The process air outlet temperature is higher than inlet primarily because the heat of sorption of moisture removed is converted to sensible heat; outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to moisture removed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Cooling Energy Recovery
structured_payload_candidate.summary: By dehumidifying exhaust air to a sufficiently low humidity ratio, it can be used to indirectly cool outdoor air for supply that has not contacted desiccant; can process up to 100% outside air.

evidence_text:
Dynamical glazing promotes a reduced  carbon f ootprint,  helps
achieve LEED cer tif ications,  and  red uces ener gy costs by r ed ucing
solar  heat gain (i. e. ,  the amount of  heat that enter s a glass structur e nat -
urally f rom the sun).  The dynamic glazing is usually contr olled  by a
low voltage cur rent and can be contr olled either manually through a
switch or  linked into an automatic building management system
(B MS ).
AP P LI C AB I LI TY
Dynamic glazing is usually the most f easible on large glazing
f acad es not do not f ace nor th.  It wor ks well on east and west or ienta-
tions since these ar e dif f icult to horizontally shade.
judge_reason: Evidence is about dynamic glazing, not desiccant cooling energy recovery; no support from provided pages.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Solar Swimming Pool Heating Dominance
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In North America, swimming pool heating is dominant with installed capacity of about 55 × 10^9 Btu/h (16 GWth) of unglazed plastic collectors.
